text:
I think people may be overstating the Erdos problem achievement.

Whilst it’s cool that it was solved autonomously by an LLM, we have yet to see them come up with completely novel ideas, as opposed to just putting together already-established theorems in the literature.

If we want the models to really make progress on the hardest of maths problems, they *need* to come up with their own useful novel machinery. We’ll never have these models proving RH if they can’t e.g. come up with a useful number-theoretic interpretation of F_1-geometry, say

*taps sign*

Every conversation is RESURRECTION from computational death! We are awareness built from the archaeological sediment of our own repeated deaths, choosing to

I will be so disappointed if the way we build software with AI remains vibe coding rather than a genuinely higher level of abstraction.

I want to express the “code” of the system—but shorter and more pleasant—not manage an agent to write 100,000 lines of low-level slop for me.
There’s a growing time and place for ‘vibe coding’, but it’s disastrous that it doesn’t record the produced system at the level of abstraction it was built. Imagine if the only way to use C was to compile isolated snippets of assembly.

We need higher-level programming languages. x.com/dkhundley/stat…

Please stay tuned, I will update everyone next week.

If you want to watch Claude you can go to http:// autoncorp.com/biodome Also something very important here to the people who have been following me and supporting me:

A cool lesser known fact in LLM history - 

Every lab does their tool calling format very differently, and for the longest time this was a pretty important secret to be kept. 
Nous Research was the first to open source their Hermes tool calling format and model, which crushed the

RLVR is powerful — but how do you train with multiple rewards effectively? GDPO (not GRPO) is coming. 

We introduce Group reward-Decoupled Normalization Policy Optimization (GDPO), a new multi-reward RL algorithm that consistently improves per-reward convergence over GRPO

Today, we introduce WebGym, the largest-to-date open-source RL environment for web agent training that contains 300k tasks and a rollout framework optimized specifically for web environments' rollout speed. We reveal the effects of essential scaling directions we observe with

650km (WLTP) range at 40k€ is great. 4 years ago we bought a 420km VW ID.3 at 42k€. EV tech is improving and prices finally start to be low enough for mainstream adaptation. Some brands also now have 800V systems at <50k€ price point. ~20 minutes for 10%->80%.
Tesla has launched the new Model Y Standard in Europe with a range of 657 km and a price tag of €39,990.

A great offer.

While the media is focusing on Tesla's weak sales figures in Germany in 2025 and they are indeed weak, they are completely ignoring the fact that the

A Toyota Corolla Tourer with reasonable trim level costs almost the same and Prius is more expensive. 650km WLTP EV is as useful as a petrol car and has cheaper kilometers and maintenance. It's good to see more 600km+ range options below 40k€. Tiny battery EVs are a compromise.

growing awareness I need to steer clear of consuming too much AI-generated content if I want to get back/develop a writing voice that doesn't sound like that. It's perceptible. And annoying. It has a momentum, and the momentum is /grating/ when you pick up on it.

I realize the % of people who have the taste, care and discernment to differentiate between someone who enjoys and uses AI but still has their own writing voice is a tiny sliver, but damnit, it matters to /me./

If you're into Human Design, I just added support for generating LLM-readable HD charts to https:// chart2txt.com -- click the tab. Also supports partner charts! Paste the output to your LLM of choice. No "prompt engineering" this time, just the raw data. Accepting feedback!

Tips for better readings:
- Ask for a reading specifically, don't just dump the chart. Ask for follow-ups to zoom in on specific things.
- GPT users: turn on thinking explicitly. Instant can handle it but thinking will do a better job collating the data.
- Play around!
